concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Directed Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two nodes with a specific direction.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a graph, representing an entity.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path in a directed graph that starts and ends at the same vertex.

concepts.name: Acyclic Graph
concepts.short_definition: A directed graph that contains no cycles.

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.short_definition: A linear ordering of vertices such that for every directed edge from vertex u to vertex v, u comes before v in the ordering.

concepts.name: Source Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A vertex with no incoming edges.

concepts.name: Sink Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A vertex with no outgoing edges.

concepts.name: In-degree
concepts.short_definition: The number of incoming edges to a vertex.

concepts.name: Out-degree
concepts.short_definition: The number of outgoing edges from a vertex.

concepts.name: Kahn's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for topological sorting using in-degrees and a queue.

concepts.name: Depth First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: DFS-based Topological Sort
concepts.short_definition: A topological sorting algorithm that uses Depth First Search.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A data structure following the First-In, First-Out (FIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A data structure following the Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each vertex has a list of its adjacent vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph using a matrix where entries indicate the presence of an edge.

concepts.name: Linear Ordering
concepts.short_definition: An arrangement of elements in a sequence.

concepts.name: Dependency
concepts.short_definition: A relationship where one task or item must be completed before another.

concepts.name: Task Scheduling
concepts.short_definition: The process of ordering tasks to be performed.

concepts.name: Course Prerequisites
concepts.short_definition: The requirement to complete certain courses before enrolling in others.

concepts.name: Build Order
concepts.short_definition: The sequence in which components of a system should be built.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: The process of identifying if a graph contains any cycles.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes that have already been processed during traversal.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the execution time of an algorithm.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the memory used by an algorithm.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Methods for storing graph data, such as adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A particular way of organizing and storing data.

concepts.name: Directed Acyclic Graph Properties
concepts.short_definition: Characteristics specific to graphs without cycles.

concepts.name: Topological Ordering Uniqueness
concepts.short_definition: Whether a topological sort for a given DAG is unique.

concepts.name: Multiple Topological Sorts
concepts.short_definition: The existence of more than one valid topological ordering for a DAG.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Systematically visiting each vertex in a graph.

concepts.name: Backtracking
concepts.short_definition: A general algorithmic technique for finding solutions by incrementally building candidates.

concepts.name: Edge Relaxation
concepts.short_definition: A step in graph algorithms where path costs are potentially updated.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path between two vertices with the minimum possible weight.

concepts.name: Longest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path between two vertices with the maximum possible weight.

concepts.name: Critical Path Method
concepts.short_definition: A project management technique for scheduling tasks.
